Title: Convergent Biochemical Pathways for Xanthine Alkaloid Production in Plants Evolved from Ancestral Enzymes with Different Catalytic Properties

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool and can form theobromine from 7-methylxanthine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 7-methylxanthine at N3 to form theobromine and methylates xanthine at N3 to form 3-methylxanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stimulant/defense-related xanthine alkaloid; the paper notes possible bioactivity of pathway intermediates on animal receptors and insect adenylate cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ancestral sequence resurrection; heterologous expression in E. coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product confirmation; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genomic and transcriptomic sequence resources used for ancestral reconstruction; GenBank and OneKP mentioned, accession numbers not specified
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro enzyme assays with SAM and xanthine alkaloid substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides ancestral catalytic capacity for xanthine methylation that could initiate the Paullinia pathway toward theobromine/caffeine; strongest activity is with 7-methylxanthine to theobromine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcAncCS1 is an ancestral Paullinia CS-type enzyme reconstructed and experimentally characterized. 2. It shows highest relative activity with 7-methylxanthine, producing theobromine via N3 methylation. 3. It also methylates xanthine to 3-methylxanthine, indicating ancestral capacity for early pathway steps.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for understanding evolutionary partitioning of methyltransferase activities and for engineering xanthine alkaloid pathway steps, but direct breeding value not assessed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ancestral enzyme does not appear to catalyze N7 methylation of tested substrates; considered a precursor to modern Paullinia CS paralogs.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: extends pathway by converting 3-methylxanthine to theobromine and also retains ancestral activities
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: theobromine and paraxanthine from 7-methylxanthine; theobromine from 3-methylxanthine; 3-methylxanthine from xanthine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Retains high activity with 7-methylxanthine to form theobromine and paraxanthine, while additionally methylating 3-methylxanthine at N7 to form theobromine and xanthine at N3 to form 3-methylxanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key evolutionary connector step, rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ancestral sequence resurrection; heterologous expression in E. coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product confirmation; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptomic sequence resources used for Paullinia lineage reconstruction; accession numbers not specified
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acquisition of N7 methylation of 3-methylxanthine enabled a connected ancestral pathway from xanthine to theobromine in Paullinia.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcAncCS2 arose after duplication of PcAncCS1. 2. It retained strong 7-methylxanthine activity but evolved higher 3-methylxanthine activity to form theobromine by N7 methylation. 3. This change likely extended the ancestral pathway to allow sequential conversion from xanthine to 3-methylxanthine to theobromine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful scaffold for engineering broader or transitional methylation activities in xanthine alkaloid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A T25S substitution in Region I largely recapitulated the descendant PcCS2-like shift toward 3-methylxanthine preference; N307Y in Region III shifts it toward PcCS1-like xanthine specificity.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool and mixed dimethylxanthine products upstream of theobromine/caffeine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: theobromine and paraxanthine from 7-methylxanthine; 1-methylxanthine and 3-methylxanthine from xanthine; theophylline from 3-methylxanthine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 7-methylxanthine at N3 and N1 to form theobromine and paraxanthine, methylates xanthine at N1 and N3 to form 1-methylxanthine and 3-methylxanthine, and methylates 3-methylxanthine at N1 to form theophylline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ancestral sequence resurrection; heterologous expression in E. coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product confirmation; kinetic analysis; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genomic and transcriptomic resources used for ancestral reconstruction; accession numbers not specified
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents an ancestral multifunctional enzyme with highest efficiency toward 7-methylxanthine but capable of producing multiple xanthine alkaloid intermediates, indicating ancestral flux different from the modern Theobroma pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcAncCS1 is an ancestral multifunctional enzyme with highest relative activity toward 7-methylxanthine. 2. It forms theobromine, paraxanthine, 1-methylxanthine, 3-methylxanthine, and theophylline depending on substrate. 3. Its catalytic profile differs from modern specialized Theobroma enzymes, indicating pathway restructuring over evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be useful as a multifunctional methyltransferase template for synthetic biology of xanthine alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely low flux toward theobromine through a single enzyme due to competing substrate specificities.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Kinetic data show kcat/KM highest with 7-methylxanthine, about fourfold higher than with xanthine or 3-methylxanthine.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms 3-methylxanthine from xanthine and theobromine from 3-methylxanthine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specialized toward N3 methylation of xanthine to form 3-methylxanthine and also catalyzes N7 methylation of 3-methylxanthine to form theobromine, with loss of ancestral N1 activities and reduced 7-methylxanthine preference.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for redirecting ancestral flux; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ancestral sequence resurrection; heterologous expression in E. coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product confirmation; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genomic and transcriptomic sequence resources used for reconstruction; accession numbers not specified
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirected ancestral catalytic flux toward the beginning of the modern Theobroma theobromine/caffeine pathway by favoring xanthine to 3-methylxanthine and enabling 3-methylxanthine to theobromine conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcAncCS2 arose after duplication of TcAncCS1 and lost the ancestral 7-methylxanthine preference. 2. It evolved highest relative activity with xanthine, converting it solely to 3-methylxanthine. 3. It also acquired N7 methylation of 3-methylxanthine to form theobromine, creating a connected pathway toward caffeine. 4. Kinetically, it is much more efficient with xanthine than with 3-methylxanthine, suggesting low ancestral flux through the second step in a single-enzyme context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme template for engineering connected xanthine-to-theobromine conversion; descendant specialization may further improve flux.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single enzyme likely provides low flux from 3-methylxanthine to theobromine because 3-methylxanthine specificity constant is nearly 40-fold lower than for xanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Region I and III mutations experimentally shifted substrate preference toward modern TcCS2- and TcCS1-like activities, respectively.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for theobromine/caffeine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Preferentially methylates xanthine at N3 to form 3-methylxanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Paullinia according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Paullinia according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization referenced from prior study; supported here by ancestral mutagenesis recapitulating PcCS1-like specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specialized upstream enzyme that efficiently supplies 3-methylxanthine for downstream theobromine formation in Paullinia.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcCS1 is a modern-day Paullinia paralog specialized for xanthine methylation. 2. It forms 3-methylxanthine, the immediate precursor for PcCS2-mediated theobromine formation. 3. Its specificity was largely recapitulated by N307Y mutation in ancestral PcAncCS2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful in pathway engineering when paired with PcCS2 to increase flux through sequential specialized steps.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core functional details in this paper are partly comparative and build on Huang et al. 2016 rather than new direct assays here.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms theobromine, an immediate precursor toward caffeine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Preferentially methylates 3-methylxanthine at N7 to form theobromine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Paullinia according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Paullinia according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization referenced from prior study; supported here by ancestral mutagenesis recapitulating PcCS2-like specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specialized downstream enzyme producing theobromine from 3-methylxanthine in Paullinia.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcCS2 is a modern-day Paullinia enzyme specialized for 3-methylxanthine. 2. It forms theobromine by N7 methylation. 3. A T25S substitution in ancestral PcAncCS2 largely recreated the PcCS2-like shift in substrate preference.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for synthetic or breeding strategies aimed at modulating theobromine/caffeine composition in Paullinia-type pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of convergent specialization with TcCS2.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for theobromine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Theobroma according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Theobroma according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization referenced from prior study; compared here with ancestral mutants and kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specialized upstream enzyme supplying 3-methylxanthine in the modern Theobroma pathway to theobromine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcCS1 is a modern-day Theobroma paralog specialized for xanthine methylation. 2. It contributes the first step in the sequential pathway to theobromine. 3. Region III substitutions in ancestral TcAncCS1 contributed to emergence of TcCS1-like preference for xanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with TcCS2 to improve pathway flux via division of labor between duplicated enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Table 1 reports kinetic parameters for TcCS1 with xanthine.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms theobromine from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Preferentially methylates 3-methylxanthine at N7 to form theobromine; also has lower activity with xanthine to form 7-methylxanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Theobroma according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with duplicated CS enzymes in Theobroma according to discussion of prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization referenced from prior study; ancestral mutagenesis in this paper reproduced TcCS2-like specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specialized downstream enzyme catalyzing theobromine formation in the modern Theobroma pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcCS2 is a modern-day Theobroma paralog specialized for 3-methylxanthine to theobromine conversion. 2. It also shows lower activity with xanthine to form 7-methylxanthine. 3. Region I substitutions in ancestral TcAncCS2 reproduced a TcCS2-like convergent shift toward 3-methylxanthine preference.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enzyme for engineering higher theobromine flux when combined with TcCS1-like upstream activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Table 1 reports kinetic parameters for TcCS2 with 3-methylxanthine.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 7-methylxanthine -> theobromine branch in Camellia CS-type pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms theobromine from 7-methylxanthine in a route used for modern caffeine production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: theobromine and paraxanthine from 7-methylxanthine; 1-methylxanthine and 3-methylxanthine from xanthine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 7-methylxanthine at N3 and N1 to form theobromine and paraxanthine; methylates xanthine to 1-methylxanthine and 3-methylxanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ancestral sequence resurrection; heterologous expression in E. coli; radiochemical enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS product confirmation; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genomic and transcriptomic resources used for reconstruction; accession numbers not specified
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents an ancient Camellia CS-type activity favoring 7-methylxanthine and producing theobromine, an enzymatic property inferred to have been maintained for over 100 My and used in modern caffeine production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAncCS is an ancestral Camellia enzyme reconstructed and characterized in vitro. 2. It shows highest activity with 7-methylxanthine and forms theobromine by N3 methylation, while also producing paraxanthine. 3. This ancient 7-methylxanthine-to-theobromine activity appears to have been retained in the Camellia lineage and contributes to modern caffeine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides an evolutionary template for engineering CS-type caffeine biosynthesis in tea-like pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses less on downstream Camellia specialization than on Paullinia and Theobroma convergence.
